channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 📍 HASC still waiting for updated E-7 Wedgetail funding request | Defense One
item.description: ‘We would like to see that budget amendment,” one senior staffer said.

item.title: 📍 Lethal humidity may be one of climate change’s most overlooked threats | ASPI Strategist
item.description: By late this century, more than 50 percent of humanity could be exposed to life-threatening heat and humidity. But one of climate change’s deadliest emerging risks remains poorly understood: lethal humidity. Climate change is not ...

item.title: 🌍 Israeli Troops Turn To Fishing Nets For Protection Against Hezbollah FPV Drones | The War Zone
item.description: Fishing nets, a staple of the Sea of Galilee going back to biblical times, are now providing protection on the modern battlefield. The post Israeli Troops Turn To Fishing Nets For Protection Against Hezbollah FPV Drones appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Cost To Link LUCAS Kamikaze Drones To Starlink Highlights Pentagon’s Ever-Growing Dependence On SpaceX | The War Zone
item.description: The Pentagon has pushed back on reports of a massive upcharge in fees, but SpaceX's products have become largely essential to U.S. operations. The post Cost To Link LUCAS Kamikaze Drones To Starlink Highlights Pentagon’s Ever-Growing Dependence On SpaceX appeared first on The…

item.title: 🌍 Tehran wants to end the war—but not at any price | Atlantic Council
item.description: Iran’s leadership is not prepared to make dramatic concessions that, in its view, would undermine the ideological foundations of the Islamic Republic. The post Tehran wants to end the war—but not at any price appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 DARPA launches search for robot medics to treat battlefield casualties | Defense News
item.description: DARPA envisions a future where "medicbots" link up to drag wounded personnel to safety, inject lifesaving drugs and form splits around broken limbs.

item.title: 🌏 Trump Is Treating Taiwan Like Collateral | Foreign Policy
item.description: The pause of a $14 billion arms package raises concerns about U.S. support for Taipei.

item.title: 🌏 Where Are The Carriers As Of May 26, 2026: Nimitz Arrives In The Caribbean | The War Zone
item.description: Meanwhile, USS George Washington departed Yokosuka, Japan, amid heightened Chinese naval activity in the Western Pacific. The post Where Are The Carriers As Of May 26, 2026: Nimitz Arrives In The Caribbean appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 SpaceX wins $2.29B to speed Space Force’s LEO communications ‘backbone’ | Breaking Defense
item.description: The SpaceX “backbone” constellation will support the broader Space Data Network, which will include the Space Development Agency’s Transport layer satellites.

item.title: 🌍 US lawmakers weigh aviation fuel cost increase from Iran war in fiscal 2027 defense hearing | Defense News
item.description: Ahead of drafting fiscal year 2027’s NDAA, U.S. lawmakers are grappling with the rise in fuel prices and what it means for the military's aviation costs.

item.title: 🏛️ House pushes Navy to nail down battleship design details | Breaking Defense
item.description: Multiple lawmakers have questioned the value of the battleship in recent weeks, often citing the expected cost of the vessel.

item.title: 🏛️ HASC $1.15T defense policy bill takes aim at industrial base challenges | Breaking Defense
item.description: The bill authorizes multiyear procurement of critical munitions, the F-35 and the Arleigh Burke destroyer.

item.title: 🌍 Another Day Of Uncertainty In The Strait After U.S. Claims Strikes On Iranian Minelayers | The War Zone
item.description: Negotiations to end the war drag on as the crisis in the Strait continues to evolve. The post Another Day Of Uncertainty In The Strait After U.S. Claims Strikes On Iranian Minelayers appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 U.S. Launches ‘Self-Defense’ Strikes on Iran | Foreign Policy
item.description: Still, Washington insists that it is committed to peace talks with Tehran.

item.title: 🌍 The Iran War Has Remade the Gulf | Foreign Policy
item.description: The region knows that Iran won the war—and is hedging its bets as a result.

item.title: 🏛️ Raytheon, Lockheed deliver first next-gen Javelin launchers to Army | Breaking Defense
item.description: The new launcher incorporates modern infrared camera technology that allows for easier and faster target detection and is smaller and lighter than the legacy unit.

item.title: 📍 Australia’s national security debate needs younger voices | ASPI Strategist
item.description: In today’s national security environment, experience alone is no longer enough. As technology, industry and strategic competition evolve at speed, Australia needs to do more than rely on established voices. It needs to actively amplify ...

item.title: 📍 An oil windfall will not fix Libya’s economy | Atlantic Council
item.description: Libya's new unified budget needs transparency, accountability, and oversight to deliver for the people. The post An oil windfall will not fix Libya’s economy appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 Kyiv hosts Belarus opposition leader as Moscow drags Minsk closer to war | Atlantic Council
item.description: As concern mounts that Moscow is pushing Minsk to join the invasion of Ukraine, Kyiv is strengthening ties with the democratic Belarusian opposition, writes Hanna Liubakova. The post Kyiv hosts Belarus opposition leader as Moscow drags Minsk closer to war appeared first on…

item.title: 🌍 Pentagon spars with SpaceX over Starlink price hike during Iran war | Defense News
item.description: The Pentagon should be paying more for access to their satellite Wi-Fi network, SpaceX officials argues.

item.title: 🌍 US launches ‘self-defense’ strikes against Iran amid ongoing ceasefire | Defense News
item.description: The U.S. military conducted military strikes Monday against Iranian missile launch sites and boats it said were attempting to lay mines.

item.title: 🏛️ Navy splits major information warfare post across three new directorates | Breaking Defense
item.description: The move follows a similar one by the Air Force nearly two years ago.

item.title: 🌏 Xi Ascendant | Foreign Policy
item.description: Trump’s Beijing trip shows that the wind is blowing in China’s direction.

item.title: 🌏 South Korea Getting Nuclear Submarines Is A Huge Deal | The War Zone
item.description: Nuclear propulsion will give South Korea more capable subs, but it will also lay the groundwork for a future sea-based nuclear deterrent option. The post South Korea Getting Nuclear Submarines Is A Huge Deal appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Why the U.S. Should Wind Down Military Aid to Israel | Foreign Policy
item.description: Washington should no longer be liable for Israeli misdeeds.

item.title: 🏴 Banned Russian Submunitions Found After Mali’s Military Announces Airstrikes | Bellingcat
item.description: This investigation is a collaboration between Bellingcat and Jeune Afrique. You can read Jeune Afrique’s article in French here. Unexploded Russian-made cluster munition bomblets, as well as damage consistent with bomblet impacts, have been found in a village in northern Mali –…

item.title: 🌏 South Korea outlines nuclear-powered submarine plan | Breaking Defense
item.description: South Korea’s Ministry of National Defense expects its first nuclear-powered submarine to enter service in the mid-2030s.

item.title: 🌏 China’s New Type 054B Frigate Joins Liaoning Carrier Group for First Time in Western Pacific | The Diplomat
item.description: Japan confirmed the first known deployment of a Jiangkai III-class frigate as part of a PLAN carrier strike group, highlighting China’s accelerating blue-water naval integration.

item.title: 📍 Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions | Just Security
item.description: A public resource tracking all the legal challenges to the Trump administration's executive orders and actions. The post Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🔐 Air Force sees another year delay for next-gen engines | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Next Generation Adaptive Propulsion program is now projected to complete prototyping work in 2031, a cumulative delay of three years compared to earlier projections.
